label: U.S. Passport Card — Name Update
description: Update your name on your U.S. passport card. A passport card is valid for land and sea travel between the U.S., Canada, Mexico, the Caribbean, and Bermuda, and is accepted as a federal photo ID domestically. The sex designation field is tracked separately — see U.S. Passport Card — Sex Designation.

process.summary: Apply simultaneously with or separately from a passport book. Use Form DS-11 if applying for the first time or if a significant name change is involved; use Form DS-82 for routine renewals. The process is identical to a passport book — you can request both on the same application.

steps.description: Complete Form DS-11 or DS-82 (same as for a passport book) and check the box indicating you also want a passport card.
steps.note: Applying for both the book and the card at the same time saves the execution fee — you only pay it once.

steps.description: Gather the same documents required for a passport book: legal name change documentation, current passport or proof of citizenship, and two passport photos.
Submit in person at a passport acceptance facility (DS-11) or by mail (DS-82). Processing timelines are the same as for a passport book.

documents_required: Current or most recent passport or passport card

access_requirements.travel_note: DS-11 requires in-person submission. DS-82 can be mailed.

process.cost: $65 for the card alone, plus the $35 execution fee for DS-11. If applying alongside a passport book, the card adds only $30 to the total.
process.estimated_time_days: 42 to 56 days routine; 14 to 21 days expedited.

presence_level_content.some_guidance: If you are already applying for a passport book, adding a passport card at the same time costs only $30 extra and saves a second in-person trip. The card is wallet-sized and useful for domestic ID purposes.

sources.label: U.S. Department of State: Passport Card
